heroHeadline: RSF Drones Strike SAF Bases in White Nile; El Fasher Detainees Include 426 Children and 22 Doctors as Drone War Escalates
heroSubtitle: A wave of RSF drone strikes on April 29 targeted three SAF military installations in White Nile State — the 18th Infantry Division headquarters in Kosti, Kenana airbase, and a Joint Forces base — extending the drone war deep into SAF-controlled rear areas south of Khartoum. The strikes follow the April 27 SAF drone attack on Al-Hamidiyah IDP camp near Zalingei (6 killed, 15 injured) and the Sudan Doctors Network's April 27 report that RSF is detaining 2,377 people in El Fasher — including 426 children, 370 women, and 22 doctors — in deplorable conditions with a cholera outbreak killing 300 detainees since February. A measles outbreak has killed ~70 and infected ~1,000 in Labado (East Darfur), and a camp fire in Central Darfur killed 4 including children on April 28. On Day 1110 of Sudan's civil war, both sides continue near-daily aerial campaigns against each other's territory; ~700 civilians were killed by drone strikes in Q1 2026 alone. The UN documents 34 million people — 65% of Sudan's population — in urgent need of humanitarian support, with only 16% of the $3 billion 2026 appeal funded.
footerNote: Data sourced from UN OCHA, UNHCR, WFP, OHCHR, WHO, ACLED, HRW, Radio Dabanga, Sudan Tribune, Reuters, Al Jazeera, MSF, Darfur24, AFP, Anadolu Agency, The National, UN News, UN Security Council, IOM, Washington Post, MadaMasr, Xinhua, Asharq Al-Awsat, Sudan Horizon, Foreign Policy, The Reporter Ethiopia, RT Africa, Abren, Pravda Sudan, Radio Tamazuj, Amnesty International, Africanews, Wikipedia (Sudan Civil War Timeline 2026), NBC News, Middle East Eye, Deutsche Welle, Daily Pakistan, The Star (Reuters), AllAfrica, Soufan Center, AP, Canada Global Affairs, Middle East Monitor, Chatham House, Emergency Lawyers Sudan, Spacewar/AFP, Democracy Now!, The New Humanitarian
